title: uniface.View
text: uniface.View The uniface.View object represents a view of a component that has been created by calling instance.createView() . Description For each instance of a dynamic server page, you can define multiple views of its data. For example, you can filter the data that is displayed, or provide alternate layouts for mobile and desktop-based browsers. A view object is created using the uniface.Instance.createView function, and is returned by uniface.Instance.getView() and .getViews() . Functions Table 1. View Functions Function Description show() Make the View visible inside the specified parent element. hide() Hide the View. Remove it from its parent element. remove() Delete the View. After this, the View object is invalid, and any attempt to call its member functions throws a uniface.InvalidDataObjectException exception. isValid() Returns true if the View is valid. Note: The View functions are not supported for the default view, which is created by the weblayout ProcScript statement, not by calling instance.createView() . Related Links Multiple Views for Dynamic Server Pages A View is an HTML layout that is displayed inside an unbound HTML element in the layout of the web page. Each instance of a dynamic server page (DSP) can have multiple views that provide different layouts for its data. createView Creates a View of the component instance with the specified name and layout. getView() Get the specified view of the component instance. getViews Returns an array of uniface.View objects representing the views of a component instance. weblayout Copies the layout of the dynamic server page instance and prepares to send it to the browser.
